error.signature: ROS2 nodes on different VLANs or subnets cannot discover each other
error.regex: (DDS.*discovery.*fail|multicast.*blocked|ROS2.*node.*not.*found|DDS.*VLAN|RTPS.*discovery.*timeout|ROS2\ nodes\ on\ different\ VLANs\ or\ subnets\ cannot\ discover\ eac)

verdict.summary: ROS2 DDS discovery relies on UDP multicast (239.255.0.1:7400). Corporate networks, VLANs, and cloud VPCs often block multicast. Nodes on different subnets can't discover each other without explicit peer configuration.

dead_ends.action: Assume ROS2 nodes on the same network will auto-discover each other
dead_ends.why_fails: DDS multicast discovery requires multicast routing between subnets. Most enterprise networks, VPNs, and cloud VPCs block multicast.

dead_ends.action: Switch DDS implementations to fix discovery issues
dead_ends.why_fails: All DDS implementations (FastDDS, CycloneDDS, Connext) use the same multicast discovery by default. Switching DDS doesn't fix network-level multicast blocking.

workarounds.action: Configure DDS peer discovery with explicit IP addresses (unicast)
workarounds.how: CycloneDDS: <Peers><Peer address='192.168.1.100'/></Peers> in cyclonedds.xml; export CYCLONEDDS_URI=cyclonedds.xml

workarounds.action: Use FastDDS Discovery Server for large networks without multicast
workarounds.how: fastdds discovery -i 0 -l 192.168.1.1 -p 11811  # run discovery server; set ROS_DISCOVERY_SERVER env var on clients

workarounds.action: Use ROS_DOMAIN_ID to isolate node groups and reduce discovery traffic
workarounds.how: export ROS_DOMAIN_ID=42  # nodes with different domain IDs don't discover each other
